paper_title: Efficacy of bacteriophages in controlling bacterial vascular wilt caused by Ralstonia solanacearum Smith on eggplants

The main field of research
Disease caused by Ralstonia solanacearum on various plant species
A soil-borne pathogen causing bacterial wilt
Using bacteriophages to control bacterial diseases
Eco-friendly method to reduce toxic chemical usage in agriculture
Promising bacteriophage isolated from eggplant used for lysis
Bacteriophage isolated from chili used for lysis
Bacteriophage isolated from daisy used for lysis
A mixture of three phages (Φ54, Φ67, and ΦBT)
Application method for phage suspension or bacteriocide
Commercial bacteriocide treatment used as a comparison
Size of lysis zone measured at 48 hours
The concentration of phages achieved in vitro
Disease incidence rate for phage ΦBT treatment at 12 days after inoculation
Severity scale recorded for phage treatments compared to control
Area Under the Disease Progress Curve used to measure disease progress
